settings.title: Five Years of Stillpoint
settings.excerpt: Our little studio turns five this spring, grown entirely by word of mouth and steady practice.
settings.body:
Five years ago we signed the lease on a small room above a bakery, with secondhand mats and no idea whether anyone would come. They did, and they kept coming.

The room is still small. The mats are better now, but the spirit of the place has not changed since those first uncertain weeks when a class of three felt like a triumph.

Grown by word of mouth

Stillpoint has never advertised. Every person on the mat is here because someone they trust mentioned us, and that quiet growth means more to us than any campaign could.

It is a slower way to build something, but it suits us. A studio full of people who came on a friend's recommendation tends to be a kind and patient one.

How we are saying thank you

To say thank you, we are offering free community classes on the last Sunday of each month through the summer, donation based, with all proceeds going to a local mental health charity.

A few things to know about the community classes:

  • They are open to everyone, including complete beginners.
  • Give what you can, or nothing at all if money is tight.
  • Every donation goes straight to the charity, not to us.

To everyone who has breathed and stretched and rested in our small room over five years, thank you. This studio is yours as much as ours.
